titleEnglish: Vibhuti Yoga — The Yoga of Divine Glories
description: Bhagavad Gita Chapter 10, Vibhuti Yoga, is one of the most celebrated chapters where Lord Krishna reveals His divine manifestations (vibhutis) to Arjuna. Krishna declares that He is the origin of all creation and that everything emanates from Him. He describes Himself as the best among all categories — Vishnu among the Adityas, the Sun among luminaries, the mind among senses, Himalaya among mountains, the Ganges among rivers, Om among words, and Rama among warriors. Arjuna, filled with devotion, requests Krishna to describe His glories in full, saying he can never tire of hearing them. This chapter teaches devotees to see the Divine in all excellence and greatness in the world, strengthening devotion through recognition of God's omnipresence. It culminates with Krishna's declaration that He sustains the entire universe with a single fragment of His being.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
भूय एव महाबाहो शृणु मे परमं वचः।
यत्तेऽहं प्रीयमाणाय वक्ष्यामि हितकाम्यया॥
verses.transliteration:
Bhuya Eva Maha-Baho Shrinu Me Paramam Vachah.
Yat-Te'ham Priyamanaya Vakshyami Hita-Kamyaya.

wordByWord.meaning: to the one who is pleased

verses.hindiTranslation: श्रीभगवान बोले — हे महाबाहो! फिर से मेरे परम वचन सुनो, जो मैं तुम्हें प्रसन्न होकर तुम्हारे हित की कामना से कहूँगा।
verses.englishTranslation: The Supreme Lord said: O mighty-armed Arjuna, listen again to My supreme word, which I shall speak to you who are pleased, desiring your welfare.
verses.commentary: Krishna opens this chapter with affection, addressing Arjuna as 'mighty-armed' and expressing that He speaks for Arjuna's benefit. This sets the tone for the chapter where Krishna will reveal His divine manifestations. The word 'bhuyah' (again) indicates that these teachings build upon what was already shared.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
न मे विदुः सुरगणाः प्रभवं न महर्षयः।
अहमादिर्हि देवानां महर्षीणां च सर्वशः॥
verses.transliteration:
Na Me Viduh Sura-Ganah Prabhavam Na Maharshayah.
Aham-Adir-Hi Devanam Maharshinam Cha Sarvashah.
verses.hindiTranslation: मेरी उत्पत्ति को न देवतागण जानते हैं, न महर्षिगण; क्योंकि मैं सब प्रकार से देवताओं और महर्षियों का आदि कारण हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Neither the hosts of gods nor the great sages know My origin, for I am the source of all the gods and great sages in every way.
verses.commentary: Krishna establishes His transcendence by declaring that even the gods and great sages cannot comprehend His origin, because He is their very source. This verse emphasizes that the Supreme is beyond the understanding of even the highest beings in creation. It invites Arjuna to approach this knowledge with humility and devotion.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
यो मामजमनादिं च वेत्ति लोकमहेश्वरम्।
असम्मूढः स मर्त्येषु सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते॥
verses.transliteration:
Yo Mam-Ajam-Anadim Cha Vetti Loka-Maheshvaram.
Asammuddhah Sa Martyeshu Sarva-Papaih Pramuchyate.

wordByWord.meaning: the great Lord of all worlds

verses.hindiTranslation: जो मुझे अजन्मा, अनादि और लोकों का महान ईश्वर जानता है, वह मनुष्यों में असम्मूढ (ज्ञानी) होकर सब पापों से मुक्त हो जाता है।
verses.englishTranslation: He who knows Me as unborn, beginningless, and the great Lord of all worlds — he, among mortals, is undeluded and freed from all sins.
verses.commentary: This verse reveals the liberating power of true knowledge about God. Recognizing Krishna as unborn and beginningless, the supreme controller of all worlds, frees one from delusion and sin. The emphasis is on the quality of knowledge — it must be free from confusion (asammudha) — indicating that intellectual understanding must be coupled with conviction.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
बुद्धिर्ज्ञानमसम्मोहः क्षमा सत्यं दमः शमः।
सुखं दुःखं भवोऽभावो भयं चाभयमेव च॥
verses.transliteration:
Buddhir-Jnanam-Asammohah Kshama Satyam Damah Shamah.
Sukham Duhkham Bhavo'bhavo Bhayam Cha-Abhayam-Eva Cha.
verses.hindiTranslation: बुद्धि, ज्ञान, असम्मोह, क्षमा, सत्य, इन्द्रिय-संयम, मन-संयम, सुख, दुःख, उत्पत्ति, विनाश, भय और अभय —
verses.englishTranslation: Intellect, knowledge, freedom from delusion, forgiveness, truth, self-control, calmness, happiness, sorrow, birth, death, fear, and fearlessness —
verses.commentary: This verse begins a list of qualities and states of being that originate from Krishna, continued in the next verse. It encompasses the full spectrum of human experience — both positive qualities like wisdom and truth, and opposite states like fear and fearlessness. Krishna claims authorship of all mental and emotional states that beings experience.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
अहिंसा समता तुष्टिस्तपो दानं यशोऽयशः।
भवन्ति भावा भूतानां मत्त एव पृथग्विधाः॥
verses.transliteration:
Ahimsa Samata Tushtis-Tapo Danam Yasho'yashah.
Bhavanti Bhava Bhutanam Matta Eva Prithag-Vidhah.
verses.hindiTranslation: अहिंसा, समता, संतोष, तप, दान, यश और अपयश — प्राणियों के ये विविध भाव मुझसे ही उत्पन्न होते हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: Non-violence, equanimity, contentment, austerity, charity, fame, and infamy — these various states of beings arise from Me alone.
verses.commentary: Completing the enumeration begun in the previous verse, Krishna asserts that all states of being — whether virtuous or otherwise — originate from Him. This is not an endorsement of negative qualities but a declaration of His role as the ultimate source of all existence. The diversity of human nature reflects the infinite creativity of the Divine.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
महर्षयः सप्त पूर्वे चत्वारो मनवस्तथा।
मद्भावा मानसा जाता येषां लोक इमाः प्रजाः॥
verses.transliteration:
Maharshayah Sapta Purve Chatvaro Manavas-Tatha.
Mad-Bhava Manasa Jata Yesham Loka Imah Prajah.
verses.hindiTranslation: सात महर्षि, उनसे भी पूर्व के चार सनकादि तथा चौदह मनु — ये सब मेरे संकल्प (मन) से उत्पन्न हुए हैं, जिनकी संतानें इस संसार की प्रजाएँ हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: The seven great sages, the four ancient ones (Sanaka etc.), and the fourteen Manus — all were born from My mind and will, and from them have come all the beings in this world.
verses.commentary: Krishna traces the cosmic lineage to Himself. The seven great sages (Saptarishis), the four Kumaras, and the Manus are the progenitors of all beings. By declaring they were born from His mind, Krishna establishes Himself as the ultimate ancestor. This connects individual existence to the Divine through an unbroken chain of creation.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
एतां विभूतिं योगं च मम यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः।
सोऽविकम्पेन योगेन युज्यते नात्र संशयः॥
verses.transliteration:
Etam Vibhutim Yogam Cha Mama Yo Vetti Tattvatah.
So'vikampena Yogena Yujyate Na-Atra Samshayah.
verses.hindiTranslation: मेरी इस विभूति (ऐश्वर्य) और योगशक्ति को जो तत्त्व से जानता है, वह अविचल भक्तियोग से युक्त हो जाता है — इसमें कोई संशय नहीं।
verses.englishTranslation: He who truly knows My divine glories and mystic power is united with Me through unshakable yoga — of this there is no doubt.
verses.commentary: This verse promises a concrete spiritual result: knowing Krishna's divine manifestations and yogic power leads to unshakable devotion. The word 'avikampena' (unshakable) emphasizes that such yoga is firm and steady, free from doubt. This knowledge is not merely intellectual but transformative, anchoring the devotee permanently in divine connection.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
अहं सर्वस्य प्रभवो मत्तः सर्वं प्रवर्तते।
इति मत्वा भजन्ते मां बुधा भावसमन्विताः॥
verses.transliteration:
Aham Sarvasya Prabhavo Mattah Sarvam Pravartate.
Iti Matva Bhajante Mam Budha Bhava-Samanvitah.
verses.hindiTranslation: मैं सबकी उत्पत्ति का कारण हूँ, मुझसे ही सब कुछ प्रवृत्त होता है — ऐसा जानकर बुद्धिमान जन भावपूर्वक मेरा भजन करते हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: I am the origin of all; from Me everything proceeds. Knowing this, the wise worship Me endowed with devotion and deep feeling.
verses.commentary: This is one of the most important verses in the Gita. Krishna declares Himself as the source of all creation — not just material but also spiritual. The wise (budha) recognize this truth and worship with genuine emotion (bhava). The combination of knowledge and devotion is highlighted — true worship arises from understanding God's supreme position.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
मच्चित्ता मद्गतप्राणा बोधयन्तः परस्परम्।
कथयन्तश्च मां नित्यं तुष्यन्ति च रमन्ति च॥
verses.transliteration:
Mach-Chitta Mad-Gata-Prana Bodhayantah Parasparam.
Kathayantash-Cha Mam Nityam Tushyanti Cha Ramanti Cha.
verses.hindiTranslation: मुझमें चित्त लगाए हुए, मुझमें प्राण अर्पित किए हुए, परस्पर एक-दूसरे को बोध कराते हुए और सदा मेरी कथाएँ करते हुए वे संतुष्ट होते हैं और आनन्द लेते हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: With their minds fixed on Me and their lives surrendered to Me, enlightening one another and constantly speaking about Me, they are content and they rejoice.
verses.commentary: Krishna describes the blissful state of His devotees. They find joy in spiritual community (satsang), sharing divine knowledge with each other. The two words 'tushyanti' (satisfaction) and 'ramanti' (delight) describe inner contentment and joyful engagement. This verse beautifully portrays the devotional lifestyle as one of fulfillment and happiness.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
तेषां सततयुक्तानां भजतां प्रीतिपूर्वकम्।
ददामि बुद्धियोगं तं येन मामुपयान्ति ते॥
verses.transliteration:
Tesham Satata-Yuktanam Bhajatam Priti-Purvakam.
Dadami Buddhi-Yogam Tam Yena Mam-Upayanti Te.
verses.hindiTranslation: जो सदा मुझमें युक्त रहते हुए प्रेमपूर्वक मेरा भजन करते हैं, उन्हें मैं वह बुद्धियोग देता हूँ जिससे वे मुझे प्राप्त करते हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: To those who are ever united with Me and worship Me with love, I give that yoga of understanding by which they come to Me.
verses.commentary: This is a profoundly comforting verse — God Himself becomes the guide for sincere devotees. The condition is constant devotion with love (priti-purvakam), and the reward is buddhi-yoga, divine understanding that leads to God-realization. It assures that God is not passive but actively helps those who seek Him with genuine love and dedication.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
तेषामेवानुकम्पार्थमहमज्ञानजं तमः।
नाशयाम्यात्मभावस्थो ज्ञानदीपेन भास्वता॥
verses.transliteration:
Tesham-Eva-Anukampa-Artham-Aham-Ajnana-Jam Tamah.
Nashayamy-Atma-Bhava-Stho Jnana-Dipena Bhasvata.
verses.hindiTranslation: उन पर कृपा करने के लिए मैं उनके हृदय में स्थित रहकर अज्ञानजनित अन्धकार को प्रकाशमान ज्ञान-दीप द्वारा नष्ट कर देता हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Out of compassion for them, I, dwelling in their hearts, destroy the darkness born of ignorance with the shining lamp of knowledge.
verses.commentary: This beautiful verse uses the metaphor of a lamp dispelling darkness. God dwells within the heart (atma-bhava-sthah) and acts from within to remove ignorance. The compassion (anukampa) of the Divine is the motivating force. This internal guidance is available to all who sincerely seek, making spiritual realization accessible through divine grace rather than mere human effort.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
परं ब्रह्म परं धाम पवित्रं परमं भवान्।
पुरुषं शाश्वतं दिव्यमादिदेवमजं विभुम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Param Brahma Param Dhama Pavitram Paramam Bhavan.
Purusham Shashvatam Divyam-Adi-Devam-Ajam Vibhum.
verses.hindiTranslation: अर्जुन बोले — आप परम ब्रह्म हैं, परम धाम हैं, परम पवित्र हैं। आप शाश्वत, दिव्य पुरुष, आदिदेव, अजन्मा और सर्वव्यापी हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: Arjuna said: You are the Supreme Brahman, the Supreme Abode, the Supreme Purifier, the Eternal Divine Person, the Primal God, Unborn and All-pervading.
verses.commentary: Arjuna now responds with a declaration of faith, affirming Krishna's supreme status. He uses multiple epithets — Param Brahma, Param Dhama, Adi-Deva — acknowledging Krishna as the highest reality. This verse marks the transition where Arjuna shifts from student to a devotee who recognizes divinity standing before him.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
आहुस्त्वामृषयः सर्वे देवर्षिर्नारदस्तथा।
असितो देवलो व्यासः स्वयं चैव ब्रवीषि मे॥
verses.transliteration:
Ahus-Tvam-Rishayah Sarve Devarshir-Naradas-Tatha.
Asito Devalo Vyasah Svayam Cha-Eva Bravishi Me.
verses.hindiTranslation: सब ऋषिगण, देवर्षि नारद, असित, देवल और व्यास — सब आपको ऐसा ही कहते हैं, और स्वयं आप भी मुझसे ऐसा कह रहे हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: All the sages declare this of You, as does the divine sage Narada, Asita, Devala, and Vyasa, and now You Yourself tell me so.
verses.commentary: Arjuna validates Krishna's self-revelation by citing the authority of revered sages — Narada, Asita, Devala, and Vyasa. This appeal to tradition (parampara) shows that Arjuna's faith is not blind but supported by the testimony of the greatest seers. The combination of scriptural authority and direct revelation makes his conviction unshakable.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
सर्वमेतदृतं मन्ये यन्मां वदसि केशव।
न हि ते भगवन्व्यक्तिं विदुर्देवा न दानवाः॥
verses.transliteration:
Sarvam-Etad-Ritam Manye Yan-Mam Vadasi Keshava.
Na Hi Te Bhagavan-Vyaktim Vidur-Deva Na Danavah.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे केशव! जो कुछ आप मुझसे कहते हैं, उस सबको मैं सत्य मानता हूँ। हे भगवान! आपके स्वरूप को न देवता जानते हैं, न दानव।
verses.englishTranslation: O Keshava, I accept as true all that You tell me. Indeed, O Lord, neither the gods nor the demons know Your manifestation.
verses.commentary: Arjuna expresses complete faith in Krishna's words. He acknowledges that even gods and demons cannot fully comprehend Krishna's nature. This verse shows the ideal attitude of a disciple — accepting the teacher's words with conviction while understanding that the Divine transcends all finite comprehension. It is faith born of wisdom, not ignorance.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
स्वयमेवात्मनात्मानं वेत्थ त्वं पुरुषोत्तम।
भूतभावन भूतेश देवदेव जगत्पते॥
verses.transliteration:
Svayam-Eva-Atmana-Atmanam Vettha Tvam Purushottama.
Bhuta-Bhavana Bhute-Sha Deva-Deva Jagat-Pate.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे पुरुषोत्तम! हे भूतभावन! हे भूतेश! हे देवदेव! हे जगत्पते! आप स्वयं ही अपने आपको अपने द्वारा जानते हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: You alone know Yourself by Your own Self, O Supreme Person, O Creator of beings, O Lord of beings, O God of gods, O Lord of the universe.
verses.commentary: Arjuna uses five magnificent epithets for Krishna, each revealing a different aspect of His supremacy. The key insight is that only God can fully know God — no finite being can comprehend the Infinite. This is not a limitation of seekers but the natural relationship between the finite and the Infinite. It makes divine revelation essential for spiritual knowledge.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
वक्तुमर्हस्यशेषेण दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः।
याभिर्विभूतिभिर्लोकानिमांस्त्वं व्याप्य तिष्ठसि॥
verses.transliteration:
Vaktum-Arhasy-Asheshena Divya Hy-Atma-Vibhutayah.
Yabhir-Vibhutibhir-Lokan-Imams-Tvam Vyapya Tishthasi.
verses.hindiTranslation: आप कृपा करके अपनी दिव्य विभूतियों का पूर्ण वर्णन कीजिए, जिन विभूतियों द्वारा आप इन सब लोकों को व्याप्त करके स्थित हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: Please describe fully Your divine glories, by which You pervade and abide in all these worlds.
verses.commentary: Arjuna makes his request explicit — he wants to hear about all of Krishna's divine manifestations. The word 'asheshena' (completely/without remainder) shows his eagerness to know everything. He recognizes that Krishna's vibhutis are the means by which the Divine pervades and sustains all worlds, making this knowledge both cosmological and devotional.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
कथं विद्यामहं योगिंस्त्वां सदा परिचिन्तयन्।
केषु केषु च भावेषु चिन्त्योऽसि भगवन्मया॥
verses.transliteration:
Katham Vidyam-Aham Yogims-Tvam Sada Parichintayan.
Keshu Keshu Cha Bhaveshu Chintyo'si Bhagavan-Maya.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे योगेश्वर! निरन्तर चिन्तन करता हुआ मैं आपको कैसे जानूँ? और हे भगवान! किन-किन भावों (वस्तुओं) में आप मेरे द्वारा चिन्तन करने योग्य हैं?
verses.englishTranslation: O Yogi, how may I know You, constantly meditating on You? In what various forms and objects are You to be contemplated by me, O Lord?
verses.commentary: Arjuna asks a practical question for devotional meditation — in which forms and things should he contemplate Krishna? This shows his desire to make every moment an act of worship. By knowing which manifestations represent Krishna, Arjuna can see the Divine everywhere and maintain constant remembrance. This question gives rise to the famous vibhuti list that follows.

verses.speaker: Arjuna
verses.devanagari:
विस्तरेणात्मनो योगं विभूतिं च जनार्दन।
भूयः कथय तृप्तिर्हि शृण्वतो नास्ति मेऽमृतम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Vistarena-Atmano Yogam Vibhutim Cha Janardana.
Bhuyah Kathaya Triptir-Hi Shrinvato Nasti Me'mritam.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे जनार्दन! अपनी योगशक्ति और विभूतियों को विस्तारपूर्वक फिर से कहिए; क्योंकि आपकी अमृतमय वाणी सुनते हुए मुझे तृप्ति नहीं होती।
verses.englishTranslation: O Janardana, tell me again in detail about Your yogic powers and glories, for I never tire of hearing Your nectarean words.
verses.commentary: This poignant verse expresses the devotee's insatiable love for hearing about God. Arjuna compares Krishna's words to amrita (nectar) — the more he hears, the more he wants. This is the nature of true divine love — it grows with every encounter rather than diminishing. The request for elaboration leads Krishna to unfold the magnificent list of His divine manifestations.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
हन्त ते कथयिष्यामि दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः।
प्राधान्यतः कुरुश्रेष्ठ नास्त्यन्तो विस्तरस्य मे॥
verses.transliteration:
Hanta Te Kathayishyami Divya Hy-Atma-Vibhutayah.
Pradhanyatah Kuru-Shreshtha Nasty-Anto Vistarasya Me.
verses.hindiTranslation: श्रीभगवान बोले — हे कुरुश्रेष्ठ! अच्छा, अब मैं तुम्हें अपनी प्रमुख दिव्य विभूतियों का वर्णन करूँगा; क्योंकि मेरे विस्तार का कोई अन्त नहीं है।
verses.englishTranslation: The Supreme Lord said: Yes, I shall describe to you My divine glories, but only the principal ones, O best of the Kurus, for there is no end to My extent.
verses.commentary: Krishna agrees to Arjuna's request but qualifies it — He will share only the principal (pradhanyatah) manifestations, since His glories are infinite and endless. The word 'hanta' conveys both willingness and affection. This practical approach makes infinite knowledge accessible by highlighting the most important examples through which the Divine can be recognized.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
अहमात्मा गुडाकेश सर्वभूताशयस्थितः।
अहमादिश्च मध्यं च भूतानामन्त एव च॥
verses.transliteration:
Aham-Atma Gudakesha Sarva-Bhuta-Ashaya-Sthitah.
Aham-Adish-Cha Madhyam Cha Bhutanam-Anta Eva Cha.

wordByWord.meaning: dwelling in the hearts of all beings

verses.hindiTranslation: हे गुडाकेश! मैं सब प्राणियों के हृदय में स्थित आत्मा हूँ तथा सब भूतों का आदि, मध्य और अन्त भी मैं ही हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: O Gudakesha, I am the Self seated in the hearts of all beings. I am the beginning, the middle, and the end of all beings.
verses.commentary: Krishna begins His vibhuti enumeration with the most fundamental truth — He is the Self (Atma) dwelling in every being's heart. He is also the beginning, sustenance, and end of all existence. This verse provides the foundational framework: every specific vibhuti that follows is an expression of this universal indwelling presence.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
आदित्यानामहं विष्णुर्ज्योतिषां रविरंशुमान्।
मरीचिर्मरुतामस्मि नक्षत्राणामहं शशी॥
verses.transliteration:
Adityanam-Aham Vishnur-Jyotisham Ravir-Amshuman.
Marichir-Marutam-Asmi Nakshatranam-Aham Shashi.
verses.hindiTranslation: आदित्यों (बारह सूर्यों) में मैं विष्णु हूँ, ज्योतियों में तेजस्वी सूर्य हूँ, मरुतों (वायुदेवताओं) में मरीचि हूँ और नक्षत्रों में चन्द्रमा हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Among the Adityas I am Vishnu; among luminaries, the radiant Sun; among the Maruts I am Marichi; and among the stars I am the Moon.
verses.commentary: The vibhuti list begins with celestial beings and luminaries. Vishnu is the greatest of the twelve Adityas, the Sun is the most prominent light, Marichi leads the wind gods, and the Moon rules the stars. Each example represents the highest or most distinguished in its category, teaching devotees to see God in all that is excellent and supreme.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
वेदानां सामवेदोऽस्मि देवानामस्मि वासवः।
इन्द्रियाणां मनश्चास्मि भूतानामस्मि चेतना॥
verses.transliteration:
Vedanam Sama-Vedo'smi Devanam-Asmi Vasavah.
Indriyanam Manash-Cha-Asmi Bhutanam-Asmi Chetana.
verses.hindiTranslation: वेदों में मैं सामवेद हूँ, देवताओं में इन्द्र हूँ, इन्द्रियों में मन हूँ और प्राणियों में चेतना (जीवनशक्ति) हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Of the Vedas I am the Sama Veda; of the gods I am Indra; of the senses I am the mind; and in living beings I am consciousness.
verses.commentary: The Sama Veda is considered the most melodious of the Vedas, Indra is the king of gods, the mind is the chief of all senses, and consciousness is the essence of life. Krishna identifies Himself with the most distinguished element in each category. The identification with consciousness (chetana) is particularly profound, as it connects God to the very principle of awareness in all life.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
रुद्राणां शङ्करश्चास्मि वित्तेशो यक्षरक्षसाम्।
वसूनां पावकश्चास्मि मेरुः शिखरिणामहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Rudranam Shankarash-Cha-Asmi Vittesho Yaksha-Rakshasam.
Vasunam Pavakash-Cha-Asmi Meruh Shikharinam-Aham.
verses.hindiTranslation: रुद्रों में मैं शंकर (शिव) हूँ, यक्षों और राक्षसों में कुबेर (वित्तेश) हूँ, वसुओं में अग्नि हूँ और पर्वतों में मेरु हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Of the Rudras I am Shankara (Shiva); of the Yakshas and Rakshasas I am Kubera (the lord of wealth); of the Vasus I am Agni (fire); and of mountains I am Meru.
verses.commentary: Krishna continues identifying Himself with the foremost in each category. Shankara (Shiva) is the greatest of the eleven Rudras, Kubera is the lord of wealth among supernatural beings, fire (Agni) is the purifier among the Vasus, and Meru is the cosmic mountain at the center of the universe. These identifications span the divine, supernatural, elemental, and geographical realms.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
पुरोधसां च मुख्यं मां विद्धि पार्थ बृहस्पतिम्।
सेनानीनामहं स्कन्दः सरसामस्मि सागरः॥
verses.transliteration:
Purodhasam Cha Mukhyam Mam Viddhi Partha Brihaspatim.
Senaninam-Aham Skandah Sarasam-Asmi Sagarah.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे पार्थ! पुरोहितों में मुझे मुख्य बृहस्पति जानो। सेनापतियों में मैं स्कन्द (कार्तिकेय) हूँ और जलाशयों में सागर (समुद्र) हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: O Partha, know Me to be Brihaspati, the chief among priests. Of generals I am Skanda (Kartikeya), and of water bodies I am the ocean.
verses.commentary: Brihaspati is the guru of the gods and foremost among priests, Skanda (Kartikeya) is the divine commander of celestial armies, and the ocean is the greatest of all water bodies. These identifications span the priestly, martial, and natural domains. Each represents supreme excellence and leadership in its field.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
महर्षीणां भृगुरहं गिरामस्म्येकमक्षरम्।
यज्ञानां जपयज्ञोऽस्मि स्थावराणां हिमालयः॥
verses.transliteration:
Maharshinam Bhrigur-Aham Giram-Asmy-Ekam-Aksharam.
Yajnanam Japa-Yajno'smi Sthavaranam Himalayah.
verses.hindiTranslation: महर्षियों में मैं भृगु हूँ, वाणी में एकाक्षर ॐ हूँ, यज्ञों में जपयज्ञ हूँ और स्थावर (अचल) पदार्थों में हिमालय हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Of the great sages I am Bhrigu; of speech I am the single syllable Om; of sacrifices I am the sacrifice of silent chanting (japa); and of immovable things I am the Himalayas.
verses.commentary: Bhrigu is the foremost of the Saptarishis, Om is the essence of all Vedic utterances, japa (silent repetition of God's name) is the purest form of sacrifice requiring no external materials, and the Himalayas are the grandest of mountains. The identification with japa-yajna is especially noteworthy as it makes divine worship accessible to all — requiring only sincere inner devotion.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
अश्वत्थः सर्ववृक्षाणां देवर्षीणां च नारदः।
गन्धर्वाणां चित्ररथः सिद्धानां कपिलो मुनिः॥
verses.transliteration:
Ashvatthah Sarva-Vrikshanam Devarshinam Cha Naradah.
Gandharvanam Chitrarath-ah Siddhanam Kapilo Munih.
verses.hindiTranslation: सब वृक्षों में मैं पीपल हूँ, देवर्षियों में नारद हूँ, गन्धर्वों में चित्ररथ हूँ और सिद्धों में कपिल मुनि हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Of all trees I am the Peepal (Ashvattha); of divine sages, Narada; of the Gandharvas, Chitraratha; and of perfected beings, the sage Kapila.
verses.commentary: The Peepal tree is sacred in Indian tradition and represents immortality. Narada is the eternal devotee who travels all three worlds spreading devotion. Chitraratha is the chief of the celestial musicians (Gandharvas), and Kapila is the founder of the Sankhya philosophy. Each represents the pinnacle of its category, showing God's presence in nature, devotion, art, and philosophy.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
उच्चैःश्रवसमश्वानां विद्धि माममृतोद्भवम्।
ऐरावतं गजेन्द्राणां नराणां च नराधिपम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Uchchaihshravasam-Ashvanam Viddhi Mam-Amritodbhavam.
Airavatam Gajendranam Naranam Cha Nara-Adhipam.
verses.hindiTranslation: घोड़ों में समुद्र-मन्थन से उत्पन्न उच्चैःश्रवा को, श्रेष्ठ हाथियों में ऐरावत को और मनुष्यों में राजा को मुझे जानो।
verses.englishTranslation: Of horses, know Me as Uchchaihshravas, born of the churning of the ocean; of lordly elephants, Airavata; and among humans, the king.
verses.commentary: Uchchaihshravas is the divine horse that emerged from the churning of the cosmic ocean, Airavata is Indra's celestial elephant, and among humans the king represents the highest authority. These vibhutis span the animal and human worlds, showing that divine excellence manifests at every level of creation. The mention of kingship connects to dharmic leadership.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
आयुधानामहं वज्रं धेनूनामस्मि कामधुक्।
प्रजनश्चास्मि कन्दर्पः सर्पाणामस्मि वासुकिः॥
verses.transliteration:
Ayudhanam-Aham Vajram Dhenunam-Asmi Kamadhuk.
Prajanash-Cha-Asmi Kandarpah Sarpanam-Asmi Vasukih.
verses.hindiTranslation: आयुधों (हथियारों) में मैं वज्र हूँ, गौओं में कामधेनु हूँ, सन्तान उत्पत्ति का कारण कामदेव हूँ और सर्पों में वासुकि हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Of weapons I am the thunderbolt; of cows, Kamadhenu (the wish-fulfilling cow); I am Kandarpa, the cause of procreation; and of serpents I am Vasuki.
verses.commentary: The vajra (thunderbolt) of Indra is the most powerful weapon, Kamadhenu fulfills all desires, Kandarpa (Cupid) represents the creative force of desire that sustains life, and Vasuki is the king of serpents. Krishna's identification with Kandarpa shows that even desire, when aligned with dharma (procreation), is a divine force.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
अनन्तश्चास्मि नागानां वरुणो यादसामहम्।
पितॄणामर्यमा चास्मि यमः संयमतामहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Anantash-Cha-Asmi Naganam Varuno Yadasam-Aham.
Pitrinam-Aryama Cha-Asmi Yamah Samyamatam-Aham.
verses.hindiTranslation: नागों में मैं शेषनाग (अनन्त) हूँ, जलचरों में वरुण हूँ, पितरों में अर्यमा हूँ और नियम से शासन करने वालों में यमराज हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Of the Nagas I am Ananta (Shesha); of aquatic beings, Varuna; of the ancestors, Aryama; and of controllers I am Yama (the god of death).
verses.commentary: Ananta (Shesha) is the infinite serpent upon whom Vishnu reclines, Varuna is the lord of the waters, Aryama is the chief of the Pitris (ancestors), and Yama enforces cosmic justice as the lord of death. These vibhutis cover the realms of the underworld, oceans, ancestral world, and the domain of cosmic law and justice.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
प्रह्लादश्चास्मि दैत्यानां कालः कलयतामहम्।
मृगाणां च मृगेन्द्रोऽहं वैनतेयश्च पक्षिणाम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Prahladash-Cha-Asmi Daityanam Kalah Kalayatam-Aham.
Mriganam Cha Mrigendro'ham Vainateyash-Cha Pakshinam.
verses.hindiTranslation: दैत्यों में मैं प्रह्लाद हूँ, गणना करने वालों में काल (समय) हूँ, पशुओं में सिंह हूँ और पक्षियों में गरुड़ हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Of the Daityas (demons) I am Prahlada; of reckoners I am Time; of animals I am the lion; and of birds I am Garuda.
verses.commentary: Prahlada, the great devotee born among demons, represents devotion triumphing over birth circumstances. Time is the ultimate calculator that governs all existence. The lion is the king of animals, and Garuda is the divine eagle, Vishnu's vehicle. The inclusion of Prahlada among demons shows that God's glory can manifest even in the most unlikely places.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
पवनः पवतामस्मि रामः शस्त्रभृतामहम्।
झषाणां मकरश्चास्मि स्रोतसामस्मि जाह्नवी॥
verses.transliteration:
Pavanah Pavatam-Asmi Ramah Shastra-Bhritam-Aham.
Jhashanam Makarash-Cha-Asmi Srotasam-Asmi Jahnavi.
verses.hindiTranslation: पवित्र करने वालों में वायु हूँ, शस्त्रधारियों में राम हूँ, मछलियों में मकर (मगरमच्छ) हूँ और नदियों में गंगा (जाह्नवी) हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Of purifiers I am the wind; of warriors I am Rama; of fishes I am the shark (Makara); and of rivers I am the Ganges.
verses.commentary: Wind purifies the atmosphere, Lord Rama is the ideal warrior-king, the Makara (shark or crocodile) is the most powerful aquatic creature, and the Ganges is the holiest of rivers. The mention of Rama as the supreme warrior is significant — it connects the avatar tradition, showing Krishna acknowledging another incarnation of Vishnu as His own divine glory.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
सर्गाणामादिरन्तश्च मध्यं चैवाहमर्जुन।
अध्यात्मविद्या विद्यानां वादः प्रवदतामहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Sarganam-Adir-Antash-Cha Madhyam Cha-Eva-Aham-Arjuna.
Adhyatma-Vidya Vidyanam Vadah Pravadatam-Aham.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे अर्जुन! सृष्टियों (रचनाओं) का आदि, अन्त और मध्य मैं हूँ। विद्याओं में अध्यात्मविद्या हूँ और वाद-विवाद करने वालों में तत्त्वनिर्णायक वाद हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: O Arjuna, of all creations I am the beginning, end, and middle. Of all sciences I am the science of the Self (Adhyatma Vidya), and of those who debate I am the logical conclusion (Vada).
verses.commentary: Krishna reaffirms His role as the totality of creation — its origin, sustenance, and dissolution. Among all forms of knowledge, the science of Self-realization (Adhyatma Vidya) is supreme because it leads to liberation. Among forms of discourse, Vada (reasoned debate aimed at truth) represents the highest, distinguishing it from sophistry or quarrelsome argument.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
अक्षराणामकारोऽस्मि द्वन्द्वः सामासिकस्य च।
अहमेवाक्षयः कालो धाताहं विश्वतोमुखः॥
verses.transliteration:
Aksharanam-Akaro'smi Dvandvah Samasikasya Cha.
Aham-Eva-Akshayah Kalo Dhata-Aham Vishvato-Mukhah.
verses.hindiTranslation: अक्षरों (वर्णों) में मैं 'अ' हूँ, समासों में द्वन्द्व समास हूँ, अक्षय (नाश न होने वाला) काल मैं ही हूँ और सब ओर मुख वाला विधाता (धाता) मैं हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Of letters I am the letter 'A'; of compound words, the dual compound (Dvandva); I am the imperishable Time, and I am the Creator facing all directions.
verses.commentary: The letter 'A' is the foundation of all alphabets and sounds — every word begins with it. The Dvandva compound preserves the identity of both words equally. Imperishable Time governs all creation. The Creator facing all directions symbolizes omniscience and omnipresence. This verse moves from linguistics to cosmic principles, showing God's presence in every domain.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
मृत्युः सर्वहरश्चाहमुद्भवश्च भविष्यताम्।
कीर्तिः श्रीर्वाक्च नारीणां स्मृतिर्मेधा धृतिः क्षमा॥
verses.transliteration:
Mrityuh Sarva-Harash-Cha-Aham-Udbhavash-Cha Bhavishyatam.
Kirtih Shrir-Vak Cha Narinam Smritir-Medha Dhritih Kshama.
verses.hindiTranslation: सबका नाश करने वाला मृत्यु मैं हूँ और भविष्य में होने वाली वस्तुओं का उद्भव (उत्पत्ति) भी मैं हूँ। स्त्रियों में (स्त्री रूप गुणों में) कीर्ति, श्री, वाणी, स्मृति, मेधा, धृति और क्षमा — ये सब मैं हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: I am the all-devouring death, and the origin of things yet to come. Among feminine qualities I am fame, prosperity, speech, memory, intelligence, steadfastness, and patience.
verses.commentary: Krishna encompasses both death and birth — the destruction of the old and the emergence of the new. The feminine qualities listed are personified as goddesses in Vedic tradition. By identifying with these, Krishna honors the divine feminine principle. Memory, intelligence, steadfastness, and patience are presented as manifestations of the Divine, elevating these qualities as sacred.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
बृहत्साम तथा साम्नां गायत्री छन्दसामहम्।
मासानां मार्गशीर्षोऽहमृतूनां कुसुमाकरः॥
verses.transliteration:
Brihat-Sama Tatha Samnam Gayatri Chhandasam-Aham.
Masanam Marga-Shirsho'ham-Ritunam Kusumakarah.
verses.hindiTranslation: सामों में बृहत्साम हूँ, छन्दों में गायत्री हूँ, महीनों में मार्गशीर्ष (अगहन) हूँ और ऋतुओं में वसन्त (कुसुमाकर) हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Of the Sama hymns I am the Brihat-Sama; of meters I am the Gayatri; of months I am Margashirsha (November-December); and of seasons I am the flower-bearing spring.
verses.commentary: The Brihat-Sama is the most melodious and celebrated Sama hymn. The Gayatri meter is the most sacred Vedic meter, used in the famous Gayatri Mantra. Margashirsha is considered the purest month of the year. Spring (Vasanta) is the most beautiful season when nature blooms. Each represents the peak of excellence in its domain.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
द्यूतं छलयतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम्।
जयोऽस्मि व्यवसायोऽस्मि सत्त्वं सत्त्ववतामहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Dyutam Chhalayatam-Asmi Tejas-Tejasvinam-Aham.
Jayo'smi Vyavasayo'smi Sattvam Sattvavatam-Aham.
verses.hindiTranslation: छल करने वालों में मैं द्यूत (जुआ) हूँ, तेजस्वियों का तेज हूँ, विजय हूँ, निश्चय (उद्यम) हूँ और सात्त्विक पुरुषों का सत्त्व (बल) हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Of the deceitful I am gambling; I am the splendor of the splendid; I am victory, I am determination, and I am the goodness of the good.
verses.commentary: Even gambling, the most cunning form of deception, is attributed to Krishna — showing that all powers, even those that seem negative, ultimately derive from the Divine. Victory, determination, and sattva (goodness) are all divine qualities. This verse demonstrates that God is the source of all power and excellence, regardless of how it is used.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
वृष्णीनां वासुदेवोऽस्मि पाण्डवानां धनञ्जयः।
मुनीनामप्यहं व्यासः कवीनामुशना कविः॥
verses.transliteration:
Vrishninam Vasudevo'smi Pandavanam Dhananjayah.
Muninam-Apy-Aham Vyasah Kavinam-Ushana Kavih.
verses.hindiTranslation: वृष्णिवंशियों में मैं वासुदेव (कृष्ण) हूँ, पाण्डवों में धनञ्जय (अर्जुन) हूँ, मुनियों में व्यास हूँ और कवियों में शुक्राचार्य (उशना कवि) हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Of the Vrishnis I am Vasudeva (Krishna); of the Pandavas, Dhananjaya (Arjuna); of sages, Vyasa; and of poets/seers, Ushana (Shukracharya).
verses.commentary: Krishna identifies Himself directly as Vasudeva among the Vrishni clan and honors Arjuna as the greatest among the Pandavas. Vyasa, the compiler of the Vedas and author of the Mahabharata, represents the pinnacle of sagehood. Shukracharya (Ushana) is the greatest among poet-seers. By naming Arjuna as His vibhuti, Krishna elevates his beloved disciple to a divine status.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
दण्डो दमयतामस्मि नीतिरस्मि जिगीषताम्।
मौनं चैवास्मि गुह्यानां ज्ञानं ज्ञानवतामहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Dando Damayatam-Asmi Nitir-Asmi Jigishatam.
Maunam Cha-Eva-Asmi Guhyanam Jnanam Jnanavatam-Aham.
verses.hindiTranslation: दण्ड देने वालों में (न्यायकर्ताओं में) दण्ड हूँ, जीतने की इच्छा रखने वालों की नीति हूँ, गोपनीय बातों में मौन हूँ और ज्ञानियों का ज्ञान हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Of punishers I am the rod of justice; of those who seek victory I am statesmanship; of secrets I am silence; and of the wise I am wisdom.
verses.commentary: Justice (danda), strategy (niti), discretion (mauna), and wisdom (jnana) represent excellence in governance, statecraft, confidentiality, and intellectual pursuits. Silence as the keeper of secrets is a profound insight — it is not emptiness but the protector of the sacred. Wisdom as the essence of the wise shows that God is not just in the person but in the quality itself.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
यच्चापि सर्वभूतानां बीजं तदहमर्जुन।
न तदस्ति विना यत्स्यान्मया भूतं चराचरम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Yach-Cha-Api Sarva-Bhutanam Bijam Tad-Aham-Arjuna.
Na Tad-Asti Vina Yat-Syan-Maya Bhutam Chara-Acharam.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे अर्जुन! जो सब प्राणियों का बीज (कारण) है, वह मैं हूँ। ऐसा कोई भी चर (गतिशील) या अचर (स्थिर) प्राणी नहीं है जो मेरे बिना हो सके।
verses.englishTranslation: O Arjuna, I am the seed of all beings. There is no being, moving or unmoving, that can exist without Me.
verses.commentary: This comprehensive verse declares Krishna as the seed (bija) — the essential cause — of all existence. Nothing in creation, whether animate or inanimate, can exist independent of Him. This is a powerful statement of divine immanence, establishing that God is not merely transcendent but intimately present in every particle of creation.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
नान्तोऽस्ति मम दिव्यानां विभूतीनां परन्तप।
एष तूद्देशतः प्रोक्तो विभूतेर्विस्तरो मया॥
verses.transliteration:
Na-Anto'sti Mama Divyanam Vibhutinam Parantapa.
Esha Tu-Uddeshatah Prokto Vibhuter-Vistaro Maya.
verses.hindiTranslation: हे परन्तप! मेरी दिव्य विभूतियों का कोई अन्त नहीं है। यह तो मैंने अपनी विभूतियों के विस्तार का संक्षेप में (संकेत रूप में) वर्णन किया है।
verses.englishTranslation: O scorcher of foes, there is no end to My divine glories. What I have told you is only a brief indication of the extent of My manifestations.
verses.commentary: Krishna concludes the main vibhuti list by reminding Arjuna that what was shared is merely indicative, not exhaustive. His divine glories are infinite and cannot be fully enumerated. The word 'uddeshatah' (by way of indication) humbly frames the entire magnificent list as just a sample. This preserves the sense of mystery and infinity that characterizes the Divine.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
यद्यद्विभूतिमत्सत्त्वं श्रीमदूर्जितमेव वा।
तत्तदेवावगच्छ त्वं मम तेजोंऽशसम्भवम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Yad-Yad Vibhutimat-Sattvam Shrimad-Urjitam-Eva Va.
Tat-Tad-Eva-Avagachchha Tvam Mama Tejo'msha-Sambhavam.
verses.hindiTranslation: जो-जो भी विभूतिमान (ऐश्वर्यशाली), श्रीमान (शोभायुक्त) और बलवान वस्तु है, उस-उसको तुम मेरे तेज के अंश से ही उत्पन्न हुई जानो।
verses.englishTranslation: Whatever being is glorious, prosperous, or powerful, know that to be born from but a fragment of My splendor.
verses.commentary: This pivotal verse gives a universal principle — wherever there is glory, beauty, or power in the world, it is but a fragment (amsha) of Krishna's divine splendor. This transforms the devotee's entire worldview, making every excellent thing in creation a reminder of God. It is a practical instruction for constant divine remembrance through perceiving excellence.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
अथवा बहुनैतेन किं ज्ञातेन तवार्जुन।
विष्टभ्याहमिदं कृत्स्नमेकांशेन स्थितो जगत्॥
verses.transliteration:
Athava Bahuna-Itena Kim Jnatena Tava-Arjuna.
Vishtabhya-Aham-Idam Kritsnam-Eka-Amshena Sthito Jagat.
verses.hindiTranslation: अथवा हे अर्जुन! इस बहुत जानने से तुम्हें क्या प्रयोजन है? मैं इस सम्पूर्ण जगत को अपने एक अंशमात्र से धारण करके (व्याप्त करके) स्थित हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: But what need is there for all this detailed knowledge, O Arjuna? I support this entire universe with a single fragment of Myself.
verses.commentary: The chapter concludes with perhaps its most awe-inspiring statement. After listing numerous vibhutis, Krishna transcends them all by declaring that the entire universe is sustained by just one fragment of His being. This final verse puts everything in perspective — all the magnificent glories described are merely a tiny expression of the infinite Divine. It evokes both humility and wonder in the devotee.
